1.title: The book bears his name
1.text: Mormon didn't just compile the record — he gave it his own name. The book you are reading is named for this prophet-general who lived through his people's destruction.

1.question: At what age did Ammaron tell young Mormon to record what he had observed about his people?

pairs.right: Hid the records and entrusted them to Mormon

1.template: the war began to be among them in the borders of Zarahemla, by the waters of {0}

1.question: What ceased among the people because of their iniquity?
options: The work of miracles and healings

1.title: Slippery treasures
1.text: The land was cursed so that buried treasures 'became slippery' — they slipped away from those who hid them. The same curse appears among other peoples in the Book of Mormon.

1.question: Whose prophetic words were fulfilled as evil spread over the land?

2.question: Why was the young Mormon appointed to lead the Nephite armies?
options: Despite his youth, he was large in stature

2.template: the Lamanites had a king, and his name was {0}

2.question: Why was Mormon's hope at the people's mourning in vain?
options: Their sorrow was not unto repentance, but the sorrowing of the damned
They were too few to fight

2.question: What did Mormon take up, according to the word of Ammaron?

2.title: Wo is me
2.text: Mormon interrupts his war chronicle with personal laments. Instead of heroism, he records the grief of leading a people who refuse to repent.

2.question: Mormon's army of thirty thousand stood against an enemy army of how many?
How were the lands divided by the treaty of the 350th year?
options: The Lamanites took the land southward; the Nephites the land northward
The land was split east and west
Zarahemla was given to the Lamanites

3.question: What did the Lord command Mormon to cry unto the people?
options: Repent, be baptized, and rebuild the church

3.question: After their victories, what sin did the Nephites fall into?
options: Boasting in their own strength and swearing to avenge themselves

3.title: 'Vengeance is mine'
3.text: The Lord's words to Mormon — 'Vengeance is mine, and I will repay' — echo Deuteronomy 32:35 and are quoted by Paul in Romans 12:19.

3.template: {0} is mine, and I will repay

3.question: After refusing to lead his people to vengeance, what did Mormon become?
options: An idle witness, recording what he saw and heard

3.question: To whom does Mormon address his plea to repent before the judgment of Christ?
options: All the ends of the earth

4.question: According to Mormon, who punishes the wicked?
options: It is by the wicked that the wicked are punished

4.question: Which city did the Lamanites attack in the 364th year, seeking to possess it?

4.title: Human sacrifice
4.text: The degradation reaches its extreme: the Lamanites begin offering Nephite women and children as sacrifices to their idols. Mormon sees in this the mark of a society with no moral restraint.

4.question: Why did the Nephites attack with exceedingly great anger in the 367th year?
options: The Lamanites had sacrificed their women and children to idols

4.template: began to be swept off by them even as a {0} before the sun

4.question: As the Lamanites were about to overthrow the land, what did Mormon do?
options: He went to the hill Shim and took up the records Ammaron had hidden

5.question: Why did the Nephites give Mormon command of their armies again?
options: They looked on him as one who could deliver them
He was the only soldier left

5.question: Why did Mormon write only a small abridgment of these events?
options: Because of the commandment he had received, and to spare readers too great sorrow

5.question: For what purpose would the record one day go to the unbelieving Jews?
options: To persuade them that Jesus is the Christ, the Son of the living God

5.title: Prophecy of the scattering
5.text: Even at the end, Mormon looks to the future: this people will be scattered, but the record will come to light through the Gentiles to convince Israel that Jesus is the Christ.

5.template: as a vessel is tossed about upon the waves, without sail or {0}
shall go forth among you as a {0}, and tear you in pieces

6.question: To which land did Mormon ask to gather his people for the final battle?
What did Mormon do with the sacred records before the last battle?
options: Hid them in the hill Cumorah, keeping a few plates for his son Moroni
Gave them all to the Lamanite king

6.question: How many of Mormon's people survived the battle at Cumorah with him?

6.title: Ten thousand each
6.text: Mormon names twenty-three commanders, each with 'his ten thousand' — roughly 230,000 dead in this account alone. The roll call turns a statistic into personal mourning.

6.question: What is the heart of Mormon's lament over his fallen people?
options: That they had departed from the Lord and rejected Jesus, who stood with open arms

6.question: Even amid his mourning, what hope does Mormon express for the dead?
options: The Eternal Father knows their state and deals with them in justice and mercy
They will be reborn on earth

7.title: Mormon's last words
7.text: This short chapter is Mormon's final message, addressed not to his contemporaries but to the future remnant of Israel who would one day read his record.

7.question: What does Mormon tell the remnant they must lay down?

7.template: first with water, then with {0} and with the Holy Ghost

8.question: Who takes up the record at the start of chapter 8?

pairs.right: Moroni's father, who made the record
The son who finishes the record

8.template: {0} years have passed away since the coming of our Lord and Savior

8.title: The three disciples
8.text: Moroni states that he and his father saw the three Nephite disciples who, according to 3 Nephi 28, would not taste death — a rare bridge between the two accounts.

8.question: Why does Moroni say the plates have 'no worth' to one who seeks them?
options: The Lord forbade that anyone should obtain them to get gain
They are made of cheap metal

8.question: According to the scripture Moroni cites, what belongs to the Lord alone?
Whose prophecies does Moroni urge the reader to search?
Moroni foresees a day when churches and their leaders would be lifted up in what?

8.template: for your {0} you shall be forgiven of your sins

8.title: He saw our day
8.text: Moroni says Jesus showed him his future readers: 'I speak unto you as if ye were present.' Verses 26–41 describe the latter days with striking specificity.

8.question: Moroni rebukes future churches for loving what more than the poor and needy?
options: Money, fine apparel, and the adorning of their churches

8.question: Moroni warns that what now hangs over the wicked who oppress the poor?

9.question: Moroni says the guilty would be more miserable dwelling with a holy God than where?
options: With the damned souls in hell

9.question: What truth about God does Moroni cite to prove He is a God of miracles?
options: He is the same yesterday, today, and forever, with no shadow of changing

9.question: According to Moroni, what came by Adam, and what came because of Christ?
options: By Adam came the fall; because of Christ came the redemption
By Adam came light; by Christ came law
By Adam came the law; by Christ came war

9.template: because that they dwindle in {0}, and depart from the right way

9.question: What signs did Jesus say would follow those who believe?
options: Casting out devils, speaking new tongues, and healing the sick

9.question: In what characters did Moroni say the record was written?

9.title: Reformed Egyptian
9.text: Moroni explains the record was engraved in 'reformed Egyptian,' and that they would have written in Hebrew had the plates allowed — a rare note on the Nephites' language.

9.question: How does Mormon's record close in its final verse?
options: With a prayer that God remember His covenant with the house of Israel
